title: Corporate Proxy and Network Configuration Support
description: Enable Jarvy to work in corporate environments with HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS proxies, custom CA certificates, and network-specific configurations
acceptance_criteria: Jarvy respects HTTP_PROXY, HTTPS_PROXY, NO_PROXY environment variables
Config file [network] section in jarvy.toml is parsed and applied
SOCKS5 proxies work for all network operations
Custom CA bundles are propagated to all tools
Basic auth credentials can be configured securely
Tool-specific proxy settings work correctly via overrides
jarvy config proxy command works for interactive configuration
Proxy settings propagate to curl, wget, git, brew, apt, npm
jarvy config proxy --test validates proxy connectivity
Proxy passwords are never logged in plain text

tasks.title: Create network module structure
tasks.description: Create src/network/mod.rs with NetworkConfig types and module organization
subtasks: Create NetworkConfig struct with http_proxy, https_proxy, socks_proxy fields
Create ProxyAuth struct with username and password
Create PasswordSource enum (Plain, Prompt, File, Env)
Create TlsConfig struct for CA bundle configuration
Create NetworkOverride struct for per-tool settings

tasks.title: Add network config parsing
subtasks: Add NetworkConfig to main Config struct
Support no_proxy as string or array

tasks.title: Implement proxy resolution
tasks.description: Create ProxyResolver that combines env vars, config, and tool overrides
subtasks: Environment variables take precedence over config
Tool-specific overrides take precedence over global config
Handle both uppercase and lowercase env vars

tasks.title: Implement credential handling
subtasks: Support password prompts with hidden input
Support reading password from environment variable
Never log credentials in plain text

tasks.title: Implement proxy propagation to child processes
tasks.description: Apply proxy settings as environment variables to spawned commands
subtasks: Set CA bundle vars (CURL_CA_BUNDLE, SSL_CERT_FILE, NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS, etc.)

tasks.title: Implement package manager helpers
tasks.description: Create helpers for configuring specific package managers
subtasks: Document which tools need explicit config vs env vars

tasks.title: Add jarvy config proxy CLI command
tasks.description: Add subcommand for interactive proxy configuration
subtasks: Support --show flag to display current config
Support --clear flag to remove proxy config
Interactive mode prompts for all settings

tasks.title: Implement proxy connectivity testing
tasks.description: Add ability to test proxy configuration
subtasks: Validate CA bundle file exists and is readable
Test connectivity to common URLs (github, npmjs, etc.)
Provide helpful error messages for failures

tasks.title: Integrate proxy into tool installation
tasks.description: Apply proxy settings throughout the tool installation flow
subtasks: Apply proxy to run() function commands
Apply proxy to custom_install script execution
Apply proxy to package manager commands
Handle per-tool overrides in tool execution

tasks.title: Write unit tests
tasks.description: Create comprehensive unit tests for network module
subtasks: Test NoProxy string vs array handling

tasks.title: Write integration tests
tasks.description: Create integration tests for proxy functionality
subtasks: Test config file parsing with [network] section
Test jarvy config proxy CLI command
Test proxy env var propagation to child processes
Test with mock proxy server (if feasible)

tasks.title: Update documentation
subtasks: Add example jarvy.toml with proxy config
Add troubleshooting guide for common proxy issues

implementation_notes.priority_order: Environment variables > Tool overrides > Global config
implementation_notes.security: Never log proxy passwords in plain text, redact in all output
implementation_notes.platform_notes: apt/dnf require config files with sudo, most tools use env vars
implementation_notes.credential_storage: Credentials stored in global ~/.jarvy/config.toml, not project jarvy.toml

risks.description: Different package managers require different proxy configuration methods
risks.mitigation: Document platform-specific behaviors, test thoroughly on all platforms

risks.description: Credential exposure in logs or error messages
risks.mitigation: Redact credentials in all logging, warn about plain-text passwords

risks.description: Custom CA certs may not work with all tools
risks.mitigation: Document CA propagation for each tool, provide troubleshooting guide
Environment variables always take precedence, preserve existing behavior

non_goals: Network diagnostics beyond basic connectivity testing
related_tasks: PRD-008 - Environment Variables Support (similar config patterns)
